initial_program:
% Determine dimensions of the grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Keep the original cells for cells that are not below the main diagonal
output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R <= C.

% For cells strictly above the main diagonal (R < C) copy them
% by swapping the coordinates when there is no nonzero cell to the right.
output(C, R, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R < C,
                       not input(R, C+1, _),
                       C < N, R < M.

% For cells on the main diagonal (R = C) copy them left if the left neighbour is empty
output(R, C-1, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
                       C > 0,
                       not input(R, C-1, _),
                       C-1 < M.

% For cells on the main diagonal copy them up if the cell above is empty
output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
                       R > 0,
                       not input(R-1, C, _),
                       R-1 < N.

% For cells strictly below the main diagonal (R > C) copy them up
% when the cell above is empty (this handles the bottomright block).
output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R > C,
                       not input(R-1, C, _),
                       R-1 >= 0.

% At most one colour may occupy any cell.
:- output(R1, C1, Color1), output(R1, C1, Color2), Color1 != Color2.

#show output/3.

Lines:

1: % Determine dimensions of the grid
2: n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
3: n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
4: row_range(N).
5: col_range(M).
6: % Keep the original cells for cells that are not below the main diagonal
7: output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R <= C.
8: 
9: % For cells strictly above the main diagonal (R < C) copy them
10: % by swapping the coordinates when there is no nonzero cell to the right.
11: output(C, R, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R < C,
12: not input(R, C+1, _),
13: R < M, C < N.
14: 
15: % For cells on the main diagonal (R = C) copy them left if the left neighbour is empty
16: output(R, C-1, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
17: C > 0,
18: not input(R, C-1, _),
19: C-1 < M.
20: 
21: % For cells on the main diagonal copy them up if the cell above is empty
22: output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
23: R > 0,
24: not input(R-1, C, _),
25: R-1 < N.
26: 
27: % For cells strictly below the main diagonal (R > C) copy them up
28: % when the cell above is empty (this handles the bottomright block).
29: output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R > C,
30: not input(R-1, C, _),
31: R-1 >= 0.
32: 
33: % At most one colour may occupy any cell.
34: :- output(R1, C1, Color1), output(R1, C1, Color2), Color1 != Color2.
35: #show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Determine dimensions of the grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Keep the original cells for cells that are not below the main diagonal
output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R <= C.

% For cells strictly above the main diagonal (R < C) copy them
% by swapping the coordinates when there is no nonzero cell to the right.
output(C, R, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R < C,
                       not input(R, C+1, _),
                       C < N, R < M.

% For cells on the main diagonal (R = C) copy them left if the left neighbour is empty
output(R, C-1, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
                       C > 0,
                       not input(R, C-1, _),
                       C-1 < M.

% For cells on the main diagonal copy them up if the cell above is empty
output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
                       R > 0,
                       not input(R-1, C, _),
                       R-1 < N.

% For cells strictly below the main diagonal (R > C) copy them up
% when the cell above is empty (this handles the bottomright block).
output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R > C,
                       not input(R-1, C, _),
                       R-1 >= 0.

% At most one colour may occupy any cell.
:- output(R1, C1, Color1), output(R1, C1, Color2), Color1 != Color2.

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Determine dimensions of the grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Keep the original cells for cells that are not below the main diagonal
output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R <= C.

% For cells strictly above the main diagonal (R < C) copy them
% by swapping the coordinates when there is no nonzero cell to the right.
output(C, R, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R < C,
                       not input(R, C+1, _),
                       C < N, R < M.

% For cells on the main diagonal (R = C) copy them left if the left neighbour is empty
output(R, C-1, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
                       C > 0,
                       not input(R, C-1, _),
                       C-1 < M.

% For cells on the main diagonal copy them up if the cell above is empty
output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R = C,
                       R > 0,
                       not input(R-1, C, _),
                       R-1 < N.

% For cells strictly below the main diagonal (R > C) copy them up
% when the cell above is empty (this handles the bottomright block).
output(R-1, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), R > C,
                       not input(R-1, C, _),
                       R-1 >= 0.

% At most one colour may occupy any cell.
:- output(R1, C1, Color1), output(R1, C1, Color2), Color1 != Color2.

#show output/3.
